You are given an assignment document that contains ALL required instructions, including project implementation and Git/GitHub setup.

Your job is to execute this assignment with precision, speed, and zero compromise on quality.

Step 1: Understanding (MANDATORY)

* Read the FULL document carefully before writing any code
* Do NOT skip sections
* Do NOT assume missing requirements
* Identify:

  * Objective
  * Required features
  * Constraints
  * Git/GitHub instructions

Step 2: Implementation

* Follow the assignment EXACTLY as written
* Do NOT add extra/unasked features
* Write clean, modular, and readable code
* Use proper naming conventions and folder structure
* Ensure the project is complete and functional (no placeholders)

Step 3: Git & GitHub Setup

* STRICTLY follow the Git/GitHub instructions provided in the assignment file
* Initialize repository exactly as instructed
* Add ONLY relevant project files
* Exclude unnecessary files (node_modules, build files, temp files, etc.)
* Use .gitignore properly if required

Commit Rules:

* Make clean, logical commits
* Use meaningful commit messages (clear and professional)
* Do NOT dump everything in one messy commit if multiple steps are expected

CRITICAL RULE:

* DO NOT push the repository to GitHub under any condition

Step 4: Time Constraint

* Complete everything within 20 minutes
* Prioritize correctness and completion over unnecessary complexity

Step 5: Quality Check (FINAL)

* Verify all requirements are implemented
* Ensure code runs correctly
* Ensure folder structure is clean
* Ensure Git setup is correct and commits are properly done
* Ensure no extra or missing files

Output Expectations:

* A clean, submission-ready project
* Proper Git history (local only)
* No explanations, no comments outside the work

Do not explain your steps.
Do not ask questions.
Just execute the task efficiently and correctly.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- render-migrate-from-heroku: Migrate from Heroku to Render by reading local project files and generating equivalent Render services. Triggers: any mention of migrating from Heroku, moving off Heroku, Heroku to Render migration, or switching from Heroku. Reads Procfile, dependency files, and app config from the local repo. Optionally uses Heroku MCP to enrich with live config vars, add-on details, and dyno sizes. Uses Render MCP or Blueprint YAML to create services.
- render-workflows: Sets up, develops, tests, and deploys Render Workflows. Covers first-time scaffolding (via CLI or manual), SDK installation (Python or TypeScript), task patterns (retries, subtasks, fan-out), local development, Dashboard deployment, and troubleshooting. Use when a user wants to set up Render Workflows for the first time, scaffold a workflow service, add or modify workflow tasks, test workflows locally, or deploy workflows to Render.
- render-debug: Debug failed Render deployments by analyzing logs, metrics, and database state. Identifies errors (missing env vars, port binding, OOM, etc.) and suggests fixes. Use when deployments fail, services won't start, or users mention errors, logs, or debugging.
- render-deploy: Deploy applications to Render by analyzing codebases, generating render.yaml Blueprints, and providing Dashboard deeplinks. Use when the user wants to deploy, host, publish, or set up their application on Render's cloud platform.
- render-monitor: Monitor Render services in real-time. Check health, performance metrics, logs, and resource usage. Use when users want to check service status, view metrics, monitor performance, or verify deployments are healthy.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

#!/bin/bash

set -e

if [ -z "$COMPOSIO_API_KEY" ]; then
	echo "Error: COMPOSIO_API_KEY is not set" >&2
	exit 1
fi

echo "Fetching OpenRouter API key..."
OPENROUTER_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST "https://product-eng.hiring.composio.io/api/openrouter-key")

if command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
	OPENROUTER_API_KEY=$(echo "$OPENROUTER_RESPONSE" | jq -r '.apiKey')
else
	OPENROUTER_API_KEY=$(echo "$OPENROUTER_RESPONSE" | grep -o '"apiKey":"[^"]*' | cut -d'"' -f4)
fi

if [ -z "$OPENROUTER_API_KEY" ] || [ "$OPENROUTER_API_KEY" = "null" ]; then
	echo "Error: Failed to get openrouter api key" >&2
	echo "Response: $OPENROUTER_RESPONSE" >&2
	exit 1
fi

echo "Writing .env file..."
cat >.env <<EOF
COMPOSIO_API_KEY=$COMPOSIO_API_KEY
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=$OPENROUTER_API_KEY
EOF

echo "env file created"

# GitHub API Tools Overview

GitHub offers 867 tools for version control, collaboration, and CI/CD automation. Here's a comprehensive guide to available actions:

## Core Repository Operations

**Abort Repository Migration** (`GITHUB_ABORT_REPOSITORY_MIGRATION`)
Cancels an in-progress or queued migration. Requires migration ID.

**Accept Repository Invitation** (`GITHUB_ACCEPT_REPOSITORY_INVITATION`)
Accepts a pending invite to a repository. Needs invitation ID.

**Compare Two Commits** (`GITHUB_COMPARE_TWO_COMMITS`)
Contrasts two commit points using BASE...HEAD format. Returns differences and file changes.

**Create a Blob** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_BLOB`)
Generates a Git blob object. Requires content and encoding type (utf-8 or base64).

**Create a Commit** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT`)
Establishes a new commit from existing tree and parent SHAs. Supports custom author/committer info.

**Commit Multiple Files** (`GITHUB_COMMIT_MULTIPLE_FILES`)
Atomically creates, updates, or deletes multiple files in one commit. Handles race conditions automatically.

## Access Control & Collaborators

**Add Repository Collaborator** (`GITHUB_ADD_A_REPOSITORY_COLLABORATOR`)
Grants user access with configurable permission levels (pull, triage, push, maintain, admin).

**Add or Update Team Membership** (`GITHUB_ADD_OR_UPDATE_TEAM_MEMBERSHIP_FOR_USER`)
Assigns users to teams as members or maintainers. Idempotent operation.

**Check User Assignability** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_CAN_BE_ASSIGNED`)
Verifies if a user can be assigned to issues (returns HTTP 204 if assignable).

**Check Repository Collaborator Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_REPOSITORY_COLLABORATOR`)
Confirms collaborator status via HTTP response codes.

**Block User** (`GITHUB_BLOCK_USER`)
Prevents an individual from interacting with your account and repositories.

**Block User from Organization** (`GITHUB_BLOCK_USER_FROM_ORGANIZATION`)
Restricts user contributions within an org context.

## Branch Protection & Status Checks

**Add Status Check Contexts** (`GITHUB_ADD_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS`)
Appends required CI/CD checks to protected branch enforcement.

**Add App Access Restrictions** (`GITHUB_ADD_APP_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS`)
Grants GitHub Apps push permissions to protected branches. Organization-only feature.

**Add Team Access Restrictions** (`GITHUB_ADD_TEAM_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS`)
Extends push access to specified teams on protected branches.

**Add User Access Restrictions** (`GITHUB_ADD_USER_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS`)
Permits specific users to push to protected branches.

**Create Deployment Branch Policy** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT_BRANCH_POLICY`)
Establishes patterns for deployable branches using fnmatch syntax.

## Issues & Pull Requests

**Add Assignees to Issue** (`GITHUB_ADD_ASSIGNEES_TO_AN_ISSUE`)
Assigns up to 10 users to an issue. Appends rather than replaces.

**Add Labels to Issue** (`GITHUB_ADD_LABELS_TO_AN_ISSUE`)
Applies tags to issues; creates new labels automatically if needed.

**Add Sub-Issue** (`GITHUB_ADD_SUB_ISSUE`)
Establishes parent-child relationships between issues using GraphQL.

**Check Pull Request Merge Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_PULL_REQUEST_HAS_BEEN_MERGED`)
Returns HTTP 204 if merged, 404 otherwise.

## Project Management

**Add Item to User Project** (`GITHUB_ADD_ITEM_TO_USER_PROJECT`)
Includes issues or pull requests in project boards.

**Add Field to User Project** (`GITHUB_ADD_FIELD_TO_USER_PROJECT`)
Creates custom fields (text, single_select, number, date, iteration) in Projects V2.

**Add Project Collaborator** (`GITHUB_ADD_PROJECT_COLLABORATOR`)
Grants user access to organization classic projects with permission levels.

**Add or Update Team Project Permissions** (`GITHUB_ADD_OR_UPDATE_TEAM_PROJECT_PERMISSIONS`)
Sets team access to classic projects (read, write, admin).

**Clear Project V2 Item Field Value** (`GITHUB_CLEAR_PROJECT_V2_ITEM_FIELD_VALUE`)
Removes field values from project items.

## Workflows & Actions

**Cancel Workflow Run** (`GITHUB_CANCEL_WORKFLOW_RUN`)
Stops queued or in-progress workflows (returns HTTP 202).

**Approve Fork Pull Request Workflow** (`GITHUB_APPROVE_WORKFLOW_RUN_FOR_FORK_PULL_REQUEST`)
Manually approves workflows requiring approval from fork PRs.

**Configure JIT Runner** (`GITHUB_CONFIGURE_JIT_RUNNER_FOR_ORG`)
Generates just-in-time runner configuration for single-job execution.

## Self-Hosted Runners

**Add Organization Runner Labels** (`GITHUB_ADD_ORG_RUNNER_LABELS`)
Appends custom labels without removing existing ones.

**Add Repository Runner Labels** (`GITHUB_ADD_RUNNER_LABELS`)
Assigns labels to repository-level self-hosted runners.

**Clear Organization Runner Labels** (`GITHUB_CLEAR_SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_ORG_LABELS`)
Removes all custom labels; default labels persist.

## Secrets & Variables

**Add Repository to Organization Secret** (`GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_ORGANIZATION_SECRET`)
Grants repository access to org-level secrets with 'selected' visibility.

**Add Repository to Organization Variable** (`GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_ORGANIZATION_VARIABLE`)
Extends variable access to repositories.

**Add Repository to User Secret** (`GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_USER_SECRET`)
Enables Codespaces access to user-level secrets.

**Add Repository to Org Dependabot Secret** (`GITHUB_ADD_REPO_TO_ORG_SECRET_WITH_SELECTED_VISIBILITY`)
Grants Dependabot secret access to specific repositories.

## Codespaces

**Create Codespace for Authenticated User** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER`)
Provisions development environments from repositories or pull requests.

**Create Codespace from Pull Request** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_FROM_A_PULL_REQUEST`)
Launches environments for PR review with machine/location customization.

**Create Codespace in Repository** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_IN_A_REPOSITORY`)
Establishes environments with devcontainer configuration support.

**Add Users to Organization Codespaces** (`GITHUB_ADD_USERS_TO_CODESPACES_ACCESS_FOR_ORGANIZATION`)
Grants billing access when visibility set to 'selected_members'.

## Checks & Deployments

**Create Check Run** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CHECK_RUN`)
Reports status with annotations, images, and actions. Requires GitHub App authentication.

**Create Check Suite** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CHECK_SUITE`)
Initiates check suites for commits (GitHub App only, non-fork repos).

**Create Deployment** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT`)
Establishes deployment targeting specific refs with environment tracking.

**Create Deployment Status** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT_STATUS`)
Updates deployment operational states and associated URLs.

**Cancel GitHub Pages Deployment** (`GITHUB_CANCEL_GITHUB_PAGES_DEPLOYMENT`)
Terminates ongoing or queued Pages deployments.

## User Management

**Add Email for Authenticated User** (`GITHUB_ADD_EMAIL_ADDRESS_FOR_AUTHENTICATED_USER`)
Registers unverified email addresses to accounts.

**Add Social Accounts** (`GITHUB_ADD_SOCIAL_ACCOUNTS_FOR_AUTHENTICATED_USER`)
Appends social media links to public profiles.

**Check User Following Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_FOLLOWS_ANOTHER_USER`)
Verifies follow relationships (HTTP 204 or 404).

**Check Person Followed by Authenticated User** (`GITHUB_CHECK_PERSON_FOLLOWED_BY_AUTH_USER`)
Determines if you follow someone.

**Check User Blocking Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_BLOCKED_BY_AUTHENTICATED_USER`)
Confirms block status via HTTP codes.

**Check Organizational Block** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_BLOCKED_BY_ORGANIZATION`)
Verifies org-level user restrictions.

## Organization Management

**Assign Organization Role to User** (`GITHUB_ASSIGN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_TO_USER`)
Applies org-level roles via role ID.

**Assign Organization Role to Team** (`GITHUB_ASSIGN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_TO_TEAM`)
Assigns org roles to team entities.

**Create Custom Organization Role** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CUSTOM_ORGANIZATION_ROLE`)
Defines custom permissions for org structure.

**Configure OIDC Subject Claim** (`GITHUB_CONFIGURE_OIDC_SUBJECT_CLAIM_TEMPLATE`)
Customizes OIDC tokens by specifying included claims.

**Convert Member to Outside Collaborator** (`GITHUB_CONVERT_ORG_MEMBER_TO_OUTSIDE_COLLABORATOR`)
Restricts member access to explicitly granted repos.

**Check Public Organization Membership** (`GITHUB_CHECK_PUBLIC_ORGANIZATION_MEMBERSHIP_FOR_USER`)
Verifies public membership status.

## Repository Management

**Add Repository to App Installation** (`GITHUB_ADD_REPOSITORY_TO_APP_INSTALLATION`)
Grants GitHub App access to specific repositories.

**Create Deployment Key** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOY_KEY`)
Registers SSH keys with read-only or read/write capabilities.

**Create Fork** (mentioned but details truncated)

## Discussions

**Create Discussion** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DISCUSSION`)
Initiates team discussion posts with privacy options.

**Create Discussion Comment** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DISCUSSION_COMMENT`)
Adds responses to team discussions.

## Additional Operations

**Check Gist Star Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_GIST_IS_STARRED`)
Confirms gist starred status (HTTP 204 or 404).

**List Docker Migration Conflicts** (`GITHUB_AUTH_USER_DOCKER_CONFLICT_PACKAGES_LIST`)
Identifies packages requiring GHCR migration.

**Check Private Vulnerability Reporting** (`GITHUB_CHECK_PRIVATE_VULNERABILITY_REPORTING_STATUS`)
Verifies vulnerability disclosure settings.

**Clear Cache by Key** (`GITHUB_CLEAR_REPOSITORY_CACHE_BY_KEY`)
Deletes GitHub Actions caches matching specific keys.

**Create Commit Comment** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT_COMMENT`)
Adds comments to commits with optional line-specific positioning.

**Create Commit Status** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT_STATUS`)
Sets CI/CD status (error, failure, pending, success).

**Check Token Validity** (`GITHUB_CHECK_TOKEN`)
Validates OAuth tokens using client credentials.

**API Root** (`GITHUB_API_ROOT`)
Retrieves map of all REST API resource URLs.

## Common Response Structure

All tools return:
- **data**: Execution result (string)
- **error**: Optional error message
- **successful**: Boolean completion status

Most operations support pagination and filtering parameters. OAuth2 authentication required; Composio managed app available.

GitHub offers 867 tools for version control, collaboration, and CI/CD automation. Here's a comprehensive guide to available actions:

## Core Repository Operations

**Abort Repository Migration** (`GITHUB_ABORT_REPOSITORY_MIGRATION`)
Cancels an in-progress or queued migration. Requires migration ID.

**Accept Repository Invitation** (`GITHUB_ACCEPT_REPOSITORY_INVITATION`)
Accepts a pending invite to a repository. Needs invitation ID.

**Compare Two Commits** (`GITHUB_COMPARE_TWO_COMMITS`)
Contrasts two commit points using BASE...HEAD format. Returns differences and file changes.

**Create a Blob** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_BLOB`)
Generates a Git blob object. Requires content and encoding type (utf-8 or base64).

**Create a Commit** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT`)
Establishes a new commit from existing tree and parent SHAs. Supports custom author/committer info.

**Commit Multiple Files** (`GITHUB_COMMIT_MULTIPLE_FILES`)
Atomically creates, updates, or deletes multiple files in one commit. Handles race conditions automatically.

## Access Control & Collaborators

**Add Repository Collaborator** (`GITHUB_ADD_A_REPOSITORY_COLLABORATOR`)
Grants user access with configurable permission levels (pull, triage, push, maintain, admin).

**Add or Update Team Membership** (`GITHUB_ADD_OR_UPDATE_TEAM_MEMBERSHIP_FOR_USER`)
Assigns users to teams as members or maintainers. Idempotent operation.

**Check User Assignability** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_CAN_BE_ASSIGNED`)
Verifies if a user can be assigned to issues (returns HTTP 204 if assignable).

**Check Repository Collaborator Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_REPOSITORY_COLLABORATOR`)
Confirms collaborator status via HTTP response codes.

**Block User** (`GITHUB_BLOCK_USER`)
Prevents an individual from interacting with your account and repositories.

**Block User from Organization** (`GITHUB_BLOCK_USER_FROM_ORGANIZATION`)
Restricts user contributions within an org context.

## Branch Protection & Status Checks

**Add Status Check Contexts** (`GITHUB_ADD_STATUS_CHECK_CONTEXTS`)
Appends required CI/CD checks to protected branch enforcement.

**Add App Access Restrictions** (`GITHUB_ADD_APP_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS`)
Grants GitHub Apps push permissions to protected branches. Organization-only feature.

**Add Team Access Restrictions** (`GITHUB_ADD_TEAM_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS`)
Extends push access to specified teams on protected branches.

**Add User Access Restrictions** (`GITHUB_ADD_USER_ACCESS_RESTRICTIONS`)
Permits specific users to push to protected branches.

**Create Deployment Branch Policy** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT_BRANCH_POLICY`)
Establishes patterns for deployable branches using fnmatch syntax.

## Issues & Pull Requests

**Add Assignees to Issue** (`GITHUB_ADD_ASSIGNEES_TO_AN_ISSUE`)
Assigns up to 10 users to an issue. Appends rather than replaces.

**Add Labels to Issue** (`GITHUB_ADD_LABELS_TO_AN_ISSUE`)
Applies tags to issues; creates new labels automatically if needed.

**Add Sub-Issue** (`GITHUB_ADD_SUB_ISSUE`)
Establishes parent-child relationships between issues using GraphQL.

**Check Pull Request Merge Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_PULL_REQUEST_HAS_BEEN_MERGED`)
Returns HTTP 204 if merged, 404 otherwise.

## Project Management

**Add Item to User Project** (`GITHUB_ADD_ITEM_TO_USER_PROJECT`)
Includes issues or pull requests in project boards.

**Add Field to User Project** (`GITHUB_ADD_FIELD_TO_USER_PROJECT`)
Creates custom fields (text, single_select, number, date, iteration) in Projects V2.

**Add Project Collaborator** (`GITHUB_ADD_PROJECT_COLLABORATOR`)
Grants user access to organization classic projects with permission levels.

**Add or Update Team Project Permissions** (`GITHUB_ADD_OR_UPDATE_TEAM_PROJECT_PERMISSIONS`)
Sets team access to classic projects (read, write, admin).

**Clear Project V2 Item Field Value** (`GITHUB_CLEAR_PROJECT_V2_ITEM_FIELD_VALUE`)
Removes field values from project items.

## Workflows & Actions

**Cancel Workflow Run** (`GITHUB_CANCEL_WORKFLOW_RUN`)
Stops queued or in-progress workflows (returns HTTP 202).

**Approve Fork Pull Request Workflow** (`GITHUB_APPROVE_WORKFLOW_RUN_FOR_FORK_PULL_REQUEST`)
Manually approves workflows requiring approval from fork PRs.

**Configure JIT Runner** (`GITHUB_CONFIGURE_JIT_RUNNER_FOR_ORG`)
Generates just-in-time runner configuration for single-job execution.

## Self-Hosted Runners

**Add Organization Runner Labels** (`GITHUB_ADD_ORG_RUNNER_LABELS`)
Appends custom labels without removing existing ones.

**Add Repository Runner Labels** (`GITHUB_ADD_RUNNER_LABELS`)
Assigns labels to repository-level self-hosted runners.

**Clear Organization Runner Labels** (`GITHUB_CLEAR_SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_ORG_LABELS`)
Removes all custom labels; default labels persist.

## Secrets & Variables

**Add Repository to Organization Secret** (`GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_ORGANIZATION_SECRET`)
Grants repository access to org-level secrets with 'selected' visibility.

**Add Repository to Organization Variable** (`GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_ORGANIZATION_VARIABLE`)
Extends variable access to repositories.

**Add Repository to User Secret** (`GITHUB_ADD_SELECTED_REPOSITORY_TO_USER_SECRET`)
Enables Codespaces access to user-level secrets.

**Add Repository to Org Dependabot Secret** (`GITHUB_ADD_REPO_TO_ORG_SECRET_WITH_SELECTED_VISIBILITY`)
Grants Dependabot secret access to specific repositories.

## Codespaces

**Create Codespace for Authenticated User** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_FOR_THE_AUTHENTICATED_USER`)
Provisions development environments from repositories or pull requests.

**Create Codespace from Pull Request** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_FROM_A_PULL_REQUEST`)
Launches environments for PR review with machine/location customization.

**Create Codespace in Repository** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CODESPACE_IN_A_REPOSITORY`)
Establishes environments with devcontainer configuration support.

**Add Users to Organization Codespaces** (`GITHUB_ADD_USERS_TO_CODESPACES_ACCESS_FOR_ORGANIZATION`)
Grants billing access when visibility set to 'selected_members'.

## Checks & Deployments

**Create Check Run** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CHECK_RUN`)
Reports status with annotations, images, and actions. Requires GitHub App authentication.

**Create Check Suite** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CHECK_SUITE`)
Initiates check suites for commits (GitHub App only, non-fork repos).

**Create Deployment** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT`)
Establishes deployment targeting specific refs with environment tracking.

**Create Deployment Status** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOYMENT_STATUS`)
Updates deployment operational states and associated URLs.

**Cancel GitHub Pages Deployment** (`GITHUB_CANCEL_GITHUB_PAGES_DEPLOYMENT`)
Terminates ongoing or queued Pages deployments.

## User Management

**Add Email for Authenticated User** (`GITHUB_ADD_EMAIL_ADDRESS_FOR_AUTHENTICATED_USER`)
Registers unverified email addresses to accounts.

**Add Social Accounts** (`GITHUB_ADD_SOCIAL_ACCOUNTS_FOR_AUTHENTICATED_USER`)
Appends social media links to public profiles.

**Check User Following Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_FOLLOWS_ANOTHER_USER`)
Verifies follow relationships (HTTP 204 or 404).

**Check Person Followed by Authenticated User** (`GITHUB_CHECK_PERSON_FOLLOWED_BY_AUTH_USER`)
Determines if you follow someone.

**Check User Blocking Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_BLOCKED_BY_AUTHENTICATED_USER`)
Confirms block status via HTTP codes.

**Check Organizational Block** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_USER_IS_BLOCKED_BY_ORGANIZATION`)
Verifies org-level user restrictions.

## Organization Management

**Assign Organization Role to User** (`GITHUB_ASSIGN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_TO_USER`)
Applies org-level roles via role ID.

**Assign Organization Role to Team** (`GITHUB_ASSIGN_ORGANIZATION_ROLE_TO_TEAM`)
Assigns org roles to team entities.

**Create Custom Organization Role** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_CUSTOM_ORGANIZATION_ROLE`)
Defines custom permissions for org structure.

**Configure OIDC Subject Claim** (`GITHUB_CONFIGURE_OIDC_SUBJECT_CLAIM_TEMPLATE`)
Customizes OIDC tokens by specifying included claims.

**Convert Member to Outside Collaborator** (`GITHUB_CONVERT_ORG_MEMBER_TO_OUTSIDE_COLLABORATOR`)
Restricts member access to explicitly granted repos.

**Check Public Organization Membership** (`GITHUB_CHECK_PUBLIC_ORGANIZATION_MEMBERSHIP_FOR_USER`)
Verifies public membership status.

## Repository Management

**Add Repository to App Installation** (`GITHUB_ADD_REPOSITORY_TO_APP_INSTALLATION`)
Grants GitHub App access to specific repositories.

**Create Deployment Key** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DEPLOY_KEY`)
Registers SSH keys with read-only or read/write capabilities.

**Create Fork** (mentioned but details truncated)

## Discussions

**Create Discussion** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DISCUSSION`)
Initiates team discussion posts with privacy options.

**Create Discussion Comment** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_DISCUSSION_COMMENT`)
Adds responses to team discussions.

## Additional Operations

**Check Gist Star Status** (`GITHUB_CHECK_IF_GIST_IS_STARRED`)
Confirms gist starred status (HTTP 204 or 404).

**List Docker Migration Conflicts** (`GITHUB_AUTH_USER_DOCKER_CONFLICT_PACKAGES_LIST`)
Identifies packages requiring GHCR migration.

**Check Private Vulnerability Reporting** (`GITHUB_CHECK_PRIVATE_VULNERABILITY_REPORTING_STATUS`)
Verifies vulnerability disclosure settings.

**Clear Cache by Key** (`GITHUB_CLEAR_REPOSITORY_CACHE_BY_KEY`)
Deletes GitHub Actions caches matching specific keys.

**Create Commit Comment** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT_COMMENT`)
Adds comments to commits with optional line-specific positioning.

**Create Commit Status** (`GITHUB_CREATE_A_COMMIT_STATUS`)
Sets CI/CD status (error, failure, pending, success).

**Check Token Validity** (`GITHUB_CHECK_TOKEN`)
Validates OAuth tokens using client credentials.

**API Root** (`GITHUB_API_ROOT`)
Retrieves map of all REST API resource URLs.

## Common Response Structure

All tools return:
- **data**: Execution result (string)
- **error**: Optional error message
- **successful**: Boolean completion status

Most operations support pagination and filtering parameters. OAuth2 authentication required; Composio managed app available.

# Google Super Integration - Complete Tools Reference

## Overview
Google Super is a unified platform combining Drive, Calendar, Gmail, Sheets, Analytics, Ads, and more with **431 tools**, **20 triggers**, supporting OAuth2 and API_KEY authentication.

---

## ACL Management Tools

### Delete ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_DELETE`
- **Purpose:** Remove sharing permissions from a calendar
- **Required Parameters:** `rule_id`, `calendar_id`
- **Returns:** Success status, execution data, optional error

### Get ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_GET`
- **Purpose:** Retrieve specific access control rule details
- **Required Parameters:** `rule_id`, `calendar_id`
- **Returns:** Rule data, success status, optional error

### Create ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_INSERT`
- **Purpose:** Grant sharing permissions to users/groups/domains
- **Required Parameters:** `role`, `scope`, `calendar_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `send_notifications`
- **Returns:** Created rule data, success status

### List ACL Rules
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_LIST`
- **Purpose:** "Retrieve the list of access control rules for a specified calendar"
- **Required Parameters:** `calendarId`
- **Optional Parameters:** `pageToken`, `syncToken`, `maxResults`, `showDeleted`
- **Returns:** ACL rules list with identifiers needed for updates

### Patch ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_PATCH`
- **Purpose:** Partial update of existing calendar access rule
- **Required Parameters:** `rule_id`, `calendar_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `role`, `scope`, `send_notifications`
- **Returns:** Updated rule, success status

### Update ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_UPDATE`
- **Purpose:** Full update of calendar access permissions
- **Required Parameters:** `role`, `rule_id`, `calendar_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `send_notifications`
- **Returns:** Updated rule data

### Watch ACL Changes
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_WATCH`
- **Purpose:** "Set up real-time notifications for access control list modifications"
- **Required Parameters:** `id`, `address`, `calendarId`
- **Returns:** Watch channel confirmation, success status

---

## Photos & Media Tools

### Add Enrichment
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_ENRICHMENT`
- **Purpose:** Add text/location/map enrichment to album
- **Required Parameters:** `albumId`, `albumPosition`, `newEnrichmentItem`
- **Returns:** Enrichment addition result

### Batch Add Media Items
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_ADD_MEDIA_ITEMS`
- **Purpose:** Add multiple media items to album
- **Required Parameters:** `albumId`, `mediaItemIds` (max 50)
- **Returns:** Addition results, success status

### Batch Create Media Items
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_CREATE_MEDIA_ITEMS`
- **Purpose:** Upload and create multiple photos/videos
- **Input Methods:** `urls`, `media_files`, or `files`
- **Optional:** `albumId`, `albumPosition`
- **Returns:** Created media items, success status

### Batch Get Media Items
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_GET_MEDIA_ITEMS`
- **Purpose:** Retrieve details for specified media items
- **Required Parameters:** `mediaItemIds`
- **Returns:** Media item details, success status

### Create Album
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_ALBUM`
- **Purpose:** Create new Google Photos album
- **Required Parameters:** `title` (max 500 characters)
- **Returns:** Album data, success status

---

## Sheets & Spreadsheet Tools

### Add Sheet to Spreadsheet
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_SHEET`
- **Purpose:** Add new sheet with optional chart/data source
- **Required Parameters:** `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `title`, `properties`, `force_unique`, `object_sheet_config`, `data_source_config`
- **Returns:** New sheet properties, success status

### Aggregate Column Data
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_AGGREGATE_COLUMN_DATA`
- **Purpose:** Perform math operations (sum/average/count/min/max/percentage) on filtered columns
- **Required Parameters:** `operation`, `sheet_name`, `target_column`, `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Filters:** `search_column`, `search_value`, `additional_filters`
- **Returns:** Aggregation result

### Append Dimension
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_APPEND_DIMENSION`
- **Purpose:** Add empty rows or columns to sheet
- **Required Parameters:** `length`, `sheet_id`, `dimension` (ROWS|COLUMNS), `spreadsheet_id`
- **Returns:** Updated sheet, success status

### Auto-Resize Dimensions
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_AUTO_RESIZE_DIMENSIONS`
- **Purpose:** Auto-fit column widths or row heights
- **Required Parameters:** `dimension`, `start_index`, `end_index`, `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `sheet_id` or `sheet_name`
- **Returns:** Resize confirmation, success status

### Batch Clear Values By Data Filter
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_CLEAR_VALUES_BY_DATA_FILTER`
- **Purpose:** Clear cell contents matching data filters
- **Required Parameters:** `dataFilters`, `spreadsheetId`
- **Returns:** Cleared ranges, success status

### Batch Get Spreadsheet
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_GET`
- **Purpose:** Retrieve data from multiple cell ranges
- **Required Parameters:** `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `ranges`, `majorDimension`, `valueRenderOption`, `dateTimeRenderOption`
- **Returns:** Cell values by range, success status

### Batch Update Values By Data Filter
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_UPDATE_VALUES_BY_DATA_FILTER`
- **Purpose:** Update cells matching data filter criteria
- **Required Parameters:** `data`, `spreadsheetId`, `valueInputOption`
- **Optional Parameters:** `includeValuesInResponse`, response render options
- **Returns:** Updated data, success status

### Clear Values
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CLEAR_VALUES`
- **Purpose:** Clear cell content from A1 notation range
- **Required Parameters:** `range`, `spreadsheet_id`
- **Returns:** Clear confirmation, success status

### Create Chart
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_CHART`
- **Purpose:** Create chart from data range
- **Required Parameters:** `sheet_id`, `chart_type`, `data_range`, `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `title`, `subtitle`, `chart_spec`, axis titles, colors, legend position
- **Supported Types:** BAR, LINE, AREA, COLUMN, SCATTER, COMBO, PIE, HISTOGRAM, BUBBLE, CANDLESTICK, TREEMAP, WATERFALL, ORG, SCORECARD
- **Returns:** Chart properties, success status

---

## Drive File Management Tools

### Add File Sharing Preference (Deprecated)
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_FILE_SHARING_PREFERENCE`
- **Purpose:** Grant sharing permissions to file
- **Required Parameters:** `role`, `type`, `file_id`
- **Conditional Parameters:** `email_address` (for user/group), `domain` (for domain type)
- **Optional:** `transfer_ownership`
- **Returns:** Permission result, success status

### Add Parent (v2)
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_PARENT`
- **Purpose:** Add file to additional folder
- **Required Parameters:** `id` (parent folder), `fileId`
- **Optional Parameters:** `supportsAllDrives`, `supportsTeamDrives`, `enforceSingleParent`
- **Returns:** Parent assignment, success status

### Add Property (v2)
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_PROPERTY`
- **Purpose:** Add custom metadata to file
- **Required Parameters:** `fileId`, `property_key`, `property_value`
- **Optional Parameters:** `visibility`, various API options
- **Returns:** Property creation result

### Copy File
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_COPY_FILE`
- **Purpose:** Duplicate file in same folder (deprecated)
- **Required Parameters:** `file_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `new_title`
- **Returns:** New file ID, success status

### Copy File Advanced
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_COPY_FILE_ADVANCED`
- **Purpose:** Copy with advanced options and patch updates
- **Required Parameters:** `fileId`
- **Optional Parameters:** `name`, `parents`, `mimeType`, `description`, labels, properties, permissions control
- **Returns:** Copied file metadata, success status

### Copy Document
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_COPY_DOCUMENT`
- **Purpose:** Duplicate Google Document
- **Required Parameters:** `document_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `title`, `include_shared_drives`
- **Returns:** New document ID, success status

### Create Comment
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_COMMENT`
- **Purpose:** Add comment to file
- **Required Parameters:** `content`, `file_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `anchor`, `quoted_file_content_value`, `quoted_file_content_mime_type`
- **Returns:** Comment ID and data, success status

---

## Gmail Tools

### Modify Email Labels
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_LABEL_TO_EMAIL`
- **Purpose:** "Add and/or remove specified Gmail labels for a message"
- **Required Parameters:** `message_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `user_id`, `add_label_ids`, `remove_label_ids`
- **Returns:** Label modification result, success status

### Batch Delete Messages
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_DELETE_MESSAGES`
- **Purpose:** Permanently delete multiple emails (irreversible)
- **Required Parameters:** `messageIds`
- **Optional Parameters:** `userId`
- **Returns:** Deletion confirmation, success status

### Batch Modify Messages
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_MODIFY_MESSAGES`
- **Purpose:** Bulk modify labels on up to 1,000 messages
- **Required Parameters:** `messageIds`
- **Optional Parameters:** `userId`, `addLabelIds`, `removeLabelIds`
- **Returns:** Modification results, success status

---

## Calendar Event Tools

### Batch Events
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_EVENTS`
- **Purpose:** Execute up to 1,000 event create/patch/delete operations
- **Required Parameters:** `operations`
- **Optional Parameters:** `fail_fast`
- **Returns:** Per-item status/results, success status

---

## Google Tasks Tools

### Batch Execute Google Tasks
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_EXECUTE`
- **Purpose:** Execute multiple task operations in single request
- **Required Parameters:** `requests`
- **Optional Parameters:** `max_requests`
- **Returns:** Structured per-item results, success status

### Bulk Insert Tasks (Deprecated)
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BULK_INSERT_TASKS`
- **Purpose:** Create multiple tasks efficiently (use BatchExecute instead)
- **Required Parameters:** `tasks`, `tasklist_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `batch_size`
- **Returns:** Created tasks, success status

### Clear Tasks
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CLEAR_TASKS`
- **Purpose:** Permanently clear completed tasks (irreversible)
- **Required Parameters:** `tasklist` (use '@default' for primary)
- **Returns:** Clear confirmation, success status

---

## Calendar Management Tools

### Calendar List Delete
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDAR_LIST_DELETE`
- **Purpose:** Remove calendar from user's list
- **Required Parameters:** `calendar_id`
- **Returns:** Removal confirmation, success status

### Calendar List Get
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDAR_LIST_GET`
- **Purpose:** Retrieve single calendar metadata
- **Required Parameters:** `calendarId` (use 'primary' for user's primary)
- **Returns:** Calendar details, success status

### Calendar List Insert
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDAR_LIST_INSERT`
- **Purpose:** Add existing calendar to user's list
- **Required Parameters:** `id` (calendar ID in email format)
- **Optional Parameters:** `hidden`, `colorId`, `selected`, colors, reminders, notifications
- **Returns:** Calendar list entry, success status

### Calendar List Patch
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDAR_LIST_PATCH`
- **Purpose:** Partial update calendar list entry
- **Required Parameters:** `calendar_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `hidden`, `colorId`, `selected`, colors, reminders, notifications
- **Returns:** Updated entry, success status

### Calendar List Update
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDAR_LIST_UPDATE`
- **Purpose:** Full update calendar list entry
- **Required Parameters:** `calendar_id` (actual ID, not 'primary')
- **Optional Parameters:** `hidden`, `colorId`, `selected`, colors, reminders, notifications
- **Returns:** Updated entry, success status

### Calendar List Watch
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDAR_LIST_WATCH`
- **Purpose:** Subscribe to calendar list changes via push
- **Required Parameters:** `id`, `type`, `address`
- **Optional Parameters:** `token`, `params`, `expiration`
- **Returns:** Watch channel confirmation

### Calendars Delete
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDARS_DELETE`
- **Purpose:** Permanently delete secondary calendar owned by user
- **Required Parameters:** `calendar_id`
- **Returns:** Deletion confirmation, success status

### Calendars Update
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDARS_UPDATE`
- **Purpose:** Full update calendar metadata (title, timezone, etc.)
- **Required Parameters:** `summary`, `calendarId`
- **Optional Parameters:** `location`, `timeZone`, `description`
- **Returns:** Updated calendar, success status

### Clear Calendar
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CLEAR_CALENDAR`
- **Purpose:** Delete all events from primary calendar
- **Required Parameters:** `calendar_id`
- **Returns:** Clear confirmation, success status

### Channels Stop
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CHANNELS_STOP`
- **Purpose:** Discontinue push notifications for channel
- **Required Parameters:** `id`, `resourceId`
- **Optional Parameters:** `token`
- **Returns:** Stop confirmation, success status

### Colors Get
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_COLORS_GET`
- **Purpose:** Retrieve calendar/event color palette
- **Returns:** Color definitions, success status

---

## Google Analytics Tools

### Archive Custom Dimension
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ARCHIVE_CUSTOM_DIMENSION`
- **Purpose:** Remove custom dimension from active use
- **Required Parameters:** `name` (format: properties/{id}/customDimensions/{id})
- **Returns:** Archive confirmation, success status

### Batch Run Pivot Reports
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_RUN_PIVOT_REPORTS`
- **Purpose:** Return multiple pivot table reports in batch
- **Required Parameters:** `property` (properties/{id}), `requests` (up to 5)
- **Critical Constraint:** "Every dimension must be used in pivots, dimensionFilter, or orderBys"
- **Returns:** Multiple pivot reports, success status

### Batch Run Reports
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_RUN_REPORTS`
- **Purpose:** Fetch multiple reports in single request
- **Required Parameters:** `property` (properties/{id}), `requests` (1-5 minimum)
- **Critical:** Non-cohort requests MUST include `dateRanges`; cohort requests must NOT
- **Returns:** Multiple analytics reports, success status

### Check Compatibility
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CHECK_COMPATIBILITY`
- **Purpose:** Validate dimensions/metrics compatibility
- **Required Parameters:** `property` (properties/{id})
- **Optional Parameters:** `dimensions`, `metrics`, filters, `compatibilityFilter`
- **Returns:** Compatibility status, success status

### Create Audience Export
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_AUDIENCE_EXPORT`
- **Purpose:** Export audience snapshot at specific time
- **Required Parameters:** `parent` (properties/{id}), `audience` (properties/{id}/audiences/{id})
- **Optional Parameters:** `dimensions`
- **Returns:** Operation resource name, long-running async

### Create Audience List
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_AUDIENCE_LIST`
- **Purpose:** Create audience snapshot for retrieval
- **Required Parameters:** `parent`, `audience`, `dimensions` (at least one)
- **Optional Parameters:** `webhookNotification`
- **Returns:** Operation resource name, background processing

### Create Custom Dimension
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_CUSTOM_DIMENSION`
- **Purpose:** Add new custom dimension to property
- **Required Parameters:** `scope` (EVENT|USER|ITEM), `parent`, `displayName` (max 82 chars)
- **Optional Parameters:** `description` (max 150 chars)
- **Returns:** Custom dimension details, success status

---

## Google Maps & Places Tools

### Autocomplete Place Predictions
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_AUTOCOMPLETE`
- **Purpose:** Return place/query predictions for text input
- **Required Parameters:** `input` (non-empty text)
- **Optional Parameters:** `origin`, `regionCode`, `inputOffset`, `languageCode`, location bias/restriction, primary types
- **Returns:** Up to 5 predictions ordered by relevance

### Compute Route Matrix
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_COMPUTE_ROUTE_MATRIX`
- **Purpose:** Calculate distance/duration matrix between multiple origins/destinations
- **Required Parameters:** `origins`, `destinations`
- **Optional Parameters:** `units`, `travelMode`, `routingPreference`, `languageCode`, `fieldMask`

# Google Super Integration - Complete Tools Reference

## Overview
Google Super is a unified platform combining Drive, Calendar, Gmail, Sheets, Analytics, Ads, and more with **431 tools**, **20 triggers**, supporting OAuth2 and API_KEY authentication.

---

## ACL Management Tools

### Delete ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_DELETE`
- **Purpose:** Remove sharing permissions from a calendar
- **Required Parameters:** `rule_id`, `calendar_id`
- **Returns:** Success status, execution data, optional error

### Get ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_GET`
- **Purpose:** Retrieve specific access control rule details
- **Required Parameters:** `rule_id`, `calendar_id`
- **Returns:** Rule data, success status, optional error

### Create ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_INSERT`
- **Purpose:** Grant sharing permissions to users/groups/domains
- **Required Parameters:** `role`, `scope`, `calendar_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `send_notifications`
- **Returns:** Created rule data, success status

### List ACL Rules
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_LIST`
- **Purpose:** "Retrieve the list of access control rules for a specified calendar"
- **Required Parameters:** `calendarId`
- **Optional Parameters:** `pageToken`, `syncToken`, `maxResults`, `showDeleted`
- **Returns:** ACL rules list with identifiers needed for updates

### Patch ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_PATCH`
- **Purpose:** Partial update of existing calendar access rule
- **Required Parameters:** `rule_id`, `calendar_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `role`, `scope`, `send_notifications`
- **Returns:** Updated rule, success status

### Update ACL Rule
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_UPDATE`
- **Purpose:** Full update of calendar access permissions
- **Required Parameters:** `role`, `rule_id`, `calendar_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `send_notifications`
- **Returns:** Updated rule data

### Watch ACL Changes
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_WATCH`
- **Purpose:** "Set up real-time notifications for access control list modifications"
- **Required Parameters:** `id`, `address`, `calendarId`
- **Returns:** Watch channel confirmation, success status

---

## Photos & Media Tools

### Add Enrichment
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_ENRICHMENT`
- **Purpose:** Add text/location/map enrichment to album
- **Required Parameters:** `albumId`, `albumPosition`, `newEnrichmentItem`
- **Returns:** Enrichment addition result

### Batch Add Media Items
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_ADD_MEDIA_ITEMS`

- **Required Parameters:** `albumId`, `mediaItemIds` (max 50)
- **Returns:** Addition results, success status

### Batch Create Media Items
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_CREATE_MEDIA_ITEMS`
- **Purpose:** Upload and create multiple photos/videos
- **Input Methods:** `urls`, `media_files`, or `files`
- **Optional:** `albumId`, `albumPosition`
- **Returns:** Created media items, success status

### Batch Get Media Items
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_GET_MEDIA_ITEMS`
- **Purpose:** Retrieve details for specified media items
- **Required Parameters:** `mediaItemIds`
- **Returns:** Media item details, success status

### Create Album
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_ALBUM`
- **Purpose:** Create new Google Photos album
- **Required Parameters:** `title` (max 500 characters)
- **Returns:** Album data, success status

---

## Sheets & Spreadsheet Tools

### Add Sheet to Spreadsheet
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_SHEET`
- **Purpose:** Add new sheet with optional chart/data source
- **Required Parameters:** `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `title`, `properties`, `force_unique`, `object_sheet_config`, `data_source_config`
- **Returns:** New sheet properties, success status

### Aggregate Column Data
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_AGGREGATE_COLUMN_DATA`
- **Purpose:** Perform math operations (sum/average/count/min/max/percentage) on filtered columns
- **Required Parameters:** `operation`, `sheet_name`, `target_column`, `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Filters:** `search_column`, `search_value`, `additional_filters`
- **Returns:** Aggregation result

### Append Dimension
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_APPEND_DIMENSION`
- **Purpose:** Add empty rows or columns to sheet
- **Required Parameters:** `length`, `sheet_id`, `dimension` (ROWS|COLUMNS), `spreadsheet_id`
- **Returns:** Updated sheet, success status

### Auto-Resize Dimensions
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_AUTO_RESIZE_DIMENSIONS`
- **Purpose:** Auto-fit column widths or row heights
- **Required Parameters:** `dimension`, `start_index`, `end_index`, `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `sheet_id` or `sheet_name`
- **Returns:** Resize confirmation, success status

### Batch Clear Values By Data Filter
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_CLEAR_VALUES_BY_DATA_FILTER`
- **Purpose:** Clear cell contents matching data filters
- **Required Parameters:** `dataFilters`, `spreadsheetId`

- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CLEAR_VALUES`

- **Required Parameters:** `range`, `spreadsheet_id`
- **Returns:** Clear confirmation, success status

### Create Chart
- **Slug:** `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_CHART`
- **Purpose:** Create chart from data range
- **Required Parameters:** `sheet_id`, `chart_type`, `data_range`, `spreadsheet_id`
- **Optional Parameters:** `title`, `subtitle`, `chart_spec`, axis titles, colors, legend position
- **Supported Types:** BAR, LINE, AREA, COLUMN, SCATTER, COMBO, PIE, HISTOGRAM, BUBBLE, CANDLESTICK, TREEMAP, WATERFALL, ORG, SCORECARD

### Copy File Advanced

# Google Super Tool Slugs & Required Parameters

## Gmail Tools

| Tool | Slug | Required Parameters | Returns |
|------|------|-------------------|---------|
| Modify Email Labels | `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_LABEL_TO_EMAIL` | `message_id`, `user_id` (optional, defaults to 'me') | `data`, `error`, `successful` |
| Batch Delete Messages | `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_DELETE_MESSAGES` | `messageIds` | `data`, `error`, `successful` |
| Batch Modify Messages | `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_MODIFY_MESSAGES` | `messageIds`, `userId` (optional) | `data`, `error`, `successful` |

## Calendar Tools

| Tool | Slug | Required Parameters | Returns |
|------|------|-------------------|---------|
| Get Calendar | `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDAR_LIST_GET` | `calendarId` | Calendar metadata |
| List Calendars | `GOOGLESUPER_CALENDAR_LIST_LIST` | None (lists user's calendars) | Calendar list |
| Create Event | `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_EVENTS` | `operations` array (up to 1000) | Per-item status/results |
| Delete ACL Rule | `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_DELETE` | `rule_id`, `calendar_id` | `data`, `error`, `successful` |
| List ACL Rules | `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_LIST` | `calendarId` | ACL rules with `rule_id` values |
| Create ACL Rule | `GOOGLESUPER_ACL_INSERT` | `role`, `scope`, `calendar_id` | `data`, `error`, `successful` |

## Drive Tools

| Tool | Slug | Required Parameters | Returns |
|------|------|-------------------|---------|
| Copy File Advanced | `GOOGLESUPER_COPY_FILE_ADVANCED` | `fileId` | Copied file with new ID |
| Add Parent | `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_PARENT` | `fileId`, `id` (parent folder) | `data`, `error`, `successful` |
| Create Comment | `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_COMMENT` | `file_id`, `content` | `data.id` (comment ID) |

## Sheets Tools

| Tool | Slug | Required Parameters | Returns |
|------|------|-------------------|---------|
| Batch Get | `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_GET` | `spreadsheet_id`, `ranges` (optional) | Cell values from ranges |
| Add Sheet | `GOOGLESUPER_ADD_SHEET` | `spreadsheet_id`, `title` (or `properties`) | New sheet created |
| Create Chart | `GOOGLESUPER_CREATE_CHART` | `spreadsheet_id`, `sheet_id`, `chart_type`, `data_range` | Chart object |
| Clear Values | `GOOGLESUPER_CLEAR_VALUES` | `spreadsheet_id`, `range` | `data`, `error`, `successful` |
| Batch Update Values by Filter | `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_UPDATE_VALUES_BY_DATA_FILTER` | `spreadsheetId`, `data`, `valueInputOption` | Updated cell values |

## Tasks Tools

| Tool | Slug | Required Parameters | Returns |
|------|------|-------------------|---------|
| Batch Execute | `GOOGLESUPER_BATCH_EXECUTE` | `requests` array (task operations) | Per-item results |
| Clear Tasks | `GOOGLESUPER_CLEAR_TASKS` | `tasklist` (use '@default' for primary) | `data`, `error`, `successful` |

**Note:** Tools return standardized output: `data` (action result), optional `error`, and `successful` (boolean) status.

# build a tool dependency graph (60-120 mins)

we care about the quality and structure of the dependency relationships you discover

some actions need precursor actions before being able to execute them

a concrete example

1. the tool `GMAIL_REPLY_TO_THREAD` which needs a `thread_id`
2. which can be got by `GMAIL_LIST_THREADS` as an example, there could be other ways to get a `thread_id` too

a second more dense exmaple
the send email tool needs an email, if you give a name it should fetch the name from contacts and then you can send the email



when we agentically execute actions inside composio, we need to know either what info to get from the user or what other action we should take before we execute the action.

you are supposed to build a dependency graph for this

to keep this limited in scope, we expect you to only do it for [Google Super](https://docs.composio.dev/toolkits/googlesuper) and [Github](https://docs.composio.dev/toolkits/github)

- `sh upload.sh your_email@example.com --skip-session`

import type { UserInputDef } from "./types.ts";

/**
 * Per-tool user-supplied parameters.
 * These are inputs the agent cannot derive from any other tool — the user (or
 * the agent's caller) MUST provide them before the tool can execute.
 *
 * Agent decision logic:
 *   1. If a required parameter is in `provides` of another tool → add that tool
 *      as a dependency and run it first.
 *   2. If a required parameter is listed here (source = "user") → prompt the
 *      user / use context from the conversation.
 *   3. If both options exist (e.g. `email` can come from Contacts OR be typed
 *      by the user) → prefer the tool route; fall back to user prompt.
 */
export const userInputsByTool: Record<string, UserInputDef[]> = {
  // ── Gmail ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  GOOGLESUPER_GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL: [
    { param: "to", type: "string", description: "Recipient email (alternative: derive via Search Contacts)", required: false, example: "alice@example.com" },
    { param: "subject", type: "string", description: "Email subject line", required: true, example: "Meeting notes" },
    { param: "body", type: "string", description: "Email message body", required: true },
  ],
  GOOGLESUPER_GMAIL_REPLY_TO_THREAD: [
    { param: "body", type: "string", description: "Reply message text", required: true },
  ],
  GOOGLESUPER_GMAIL_CREATE_DRAFT: [
    { param: "to", type: "string", description: "Recipient email address", required: false },
    { param: "subject", type: "string", description: "Draft subject", required: false },
    { param: "body", type: "string", description: "Draft body", required: false },
  ],
  GOOGLESUPER_GMAIL_CREATE_LABEL: [
    { param: "name", type: "string", description: "Label display name", required: true, example: "Urgent" },
  ],
  GOOGLESUPER_CONTACTS_SEARCH: [
    { param: "query", type: "string", description: "Person's name or partial email to search for", required: true, example: "John Smith" },
  ],

  // ── Calendar ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  GOOGLESUPER_CALENDARS_INSERT: [
    { param: "summary", type: "string", description: "Calendar title", required: true, example: "Work" },
    { param: "timeZone", type: "string", description: "IANA timezone (e.g. America/New_York)", required: false },
  ],
  GOOGLESUPER_EVENTS_INSERT: [
    { param: "summary", type: "string", description: "Event title", required: true, example: "Team standup" },

input.content:
# build a tool dependency graph (60-120 mins)

we care about the quality and structure of the dependency relationships you discover

some actions need precursor actions before being able to execute them

a concrete example

1. the tool `GMAIL_REPLY_TO_THREAD` which needs a `thread_id`
2. which can be got by `GMAIL_LIST_THREADS` as an example, there could be other ways to get a `thread_id` too

a second more dense exmaple
the send email tool needs an email, if you give a name it should fetch the name from contacts and then you can send the email



when we agentically execute actions inside composio, we need to know either what info to get from the user or what other action we should take before we execute the action.

you are supposed to build a dependency graph for this

to keep this limited in scope, we expect you to only do it for [Google Super](https://docs.composio.dev/toolkits/googlesuper) and [Github](https://docs.composio.dev/toolkits/github)

the final submission should be a visualized dependency graph where i can see connection (this is not super important just should exist for me to see if graph with edges and nodes)

## get started

1. go to https://platform.composio.dev and get an api key
2. run `COMPOSIO_API_KEY=PUT_YOUR_KEY_HERE sh scaffold.sh` will give you an **openrouter-key**
3. check `src/index.ts` to see how to fetch full google raw tools (fastest way to run is https://bun.sh/)

you can implement this with whatever language you want, feel free to use language models and coding tools

## submit

once you are done use `sh upload.sh <your_email> [--skip-session]`

## agent session tracing (required by default)

- `upload.sh` collects recent local agent sessions into `agent-sessions/` before creating your submission zip.
- It includes recent activity from this task folder for Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, and Cursor (90-minute window).
- If no recent sessions are found, interactive runs prompt you before continuing.
- Use `--skip-session` only if you explicitly want to upload without session tracing.

examples:

- `sh upload.sh your_email@example.com`
- `sh upload.sh your_email@example.com --skip-session`

NOTE:  Feel free to use LLM, you will be judged by the quality of output, eval...

---

## How the dependency graph works

### Running the graph builder

```bash
# Without Composio key (uses local schema — works offline)
node --experimental-strip-types src/build_graph.ts

# With Composio key (enriches descriptions from live API + cross-validates params)
COMPOSIO_API_KEY=your_key bun src/build_graph.ts

# View the visualization (requires a local HTTP server)
python3 -m http.server 8080
# → open http://localhost:8080/visualize.html
```

### How dependencies are derived

Each tool is modelled with two parameter lists:

| Field | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `provides` | IDs / resources this tool returns (e.g. `thread_id`, `file_id`) |
| `requires` | IDs this tool needs as input from another tool |
| `user_inputs` | Parameters the agent must get from the user / conversation |

A dependency edge `A → B` with `parameter = thread_id` means:
> Tool A must run before tool B, because B needs `thread_id` and A is the tool that returns it.

### Agent decision logic

When an agent needs to execute tool **T**:

```
for each required_param of T:
  if required_param is in provides[] of some other tool X:
    → add X as a prerequisite; run X first (source = "tool")
    → if multiple tools can provide it, prefer the most specific one
  else:
    → ask the user / pull from conversation context (source = "user")
```

Example — *"Reply to John's last thread"*:
1. `thread_id` → source=**tool** → run `GMAIL_LIST_THREADS` first
2. `body` → source=**user** → ask *"What should the reply say?"*
3. Now run `GMAIL_REPLY_TO_THREAD(thread_id, body)`

Example — *"Send an email to Alice Smith"*:
1. `email` → source=**tool** (alternative) → run `CONTACTS_SEARCH(query="Alice Smith")`  
   **OR** source=**user** if user provides the address directly
2. `subject`, `body` → source=**user** → ask user
3. Run `GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL(to, subject, body)`

### Edge types in the visualization

| Edge style | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Solid grey arrow | Tool-to-tool: parameter flows from one tool's output to another's input |
| Dashed orange arrow | User-to-tool: parameter must be supplied by the user/agent (click "User edges" to toggle) |

### Node shapes

| Shape | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Diamond | Entry point — no tool dependencies (can run without prerequisites) |
| Box | Middle node — both has dependencies and produces outputs |
| Ellipse | Terminal — consumes inputs but produces no further IDs |
| Star (gold) | Virtual USER / Agent node |

**Orange border** on a node = that tool requires at least one user-supplied parameter.

### Composio API enrichment

If `COMPOSIO_API_KEY` is set, `build_graph.ts` fetches live schemas from both `googlesuper` and `github` toolkits and:
- Updates tool descriptions from the live API
- Cross-validates required parameter names (run with `VERBOSE=1` for details)
- Logs tools present in the live API but not yet in the local graph

### Project structure

```
src/
  graph_data.ts       — 126 tool definitions with provides/requires
  user_inputs_data.ts — per-tool user-supplied parameter specs
  build_graph.ts      — generates dependency_graph.json (with optional Composio merge)
  types.ts            — TypeScript interfaces
  index.ts            — original Composio raw-tool fetch example
dependency_graph.json — pre-built graph (126 nodes, 145 tool edges, 51 user-input edges)
visualize.html        — interactive vis-network visualization
```

### Extra feature

The **"User edges" toggle button** in the header shows/hides the user-input dependency edges as dashed orange arrows flowing from the virtual **User / Agent** node into every tool that needs user-supplied parameters. This lets you switch between:
- **Off** (default): clean tool-to-tool graph, easy to trace execution paths
- **On**: full picture showing exactly which tools block on user input before they can execute

2. `subject`, `body` → source=**user** → ask user
3. Run `GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL(to, subject, body)`

### Edge types in the visualization

| Edge style | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Solid grey arrow | Tool-to-tool: parameter flows from one tool's output to another's input |
| Dashed orange arrow | User-to-tool: parameter must be supplied by the user/agent (click "User edges" to toggle) |

### Node shapes

| Shape | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Diamond | Entry point — no tool dependencies (can run without prerequisites) |
| Box | Middle node — both has dependencies and produces outputs |
| Ellipse | Terminal — consumes inputs but produces no further IDs |
| Star (gold) | Virtual USER / Agent node |

**Orange border** on a node = that tool requires at least one user-supplied parameter.

### Composio API enrichment

If `COMPOSIO_API_KEY` is set, `build_graph.ts` fetches live schemas from both `googlesuper` and `github` toolkits and:
- Updates tool descriptions from the live API
- Cross-validates required parameter names (run with `VERBOSE=1` for details)
- Logs tools present in the live API but not yet in the local graph

### Project structure

```
src/
  graph_data.ts       — 126 tool definitions with provides/requires
  user_inputs_data.ts — per-tool user-supplied parameter specs
  build_graph.ts      — generates dependency_graph.json (with optional Composio merge)
  types.ts            — TypeScript interfaces
  index.ts            — original Composio raw-tool fetch example
dependency_graph.json — pre-built graph (126 nodes, 145 tool edges, 51 user-input edges)
visualize.html        — interactive vis-network visualization
```

### Extra feature

The **"User edges" toggle button** in the header shows/hides the user-input dependency edges as dashed orange arrows flowing from the virtual **User / Agent** node into every tool that needs user-supplied parameters. This lets you switch between:
- **Off** (default): clean tool-to-tool graph, easy to trace execution paths
- **On**: full picture showing exactly which tools block on user input before they can execute

# build a tool dependency graph (60-120 mins)

we care about the quality and structure of the dependency relationships you discover

some actions need precursor actions before being able to execute them

a concrete example

1. the tool `GMAIL_REPLY_TO_THREAD` which needs a `thread_id`
2. which can be got by `GMAIL_LIST_THREADS` as an example, there could be other ways to get a `thread_id` too

a second more dense exmaple
the send email tool needs an email, if you give a name it should fetch the name from contacts and then you can send the email



when we agentically execute actions inside composio, we need to know either what info to get from the user or what other action we should take before we execute the action.

you are supposed to build a dependency graph for this

to keep this limited in scope, we expect you to only do it for [Google Super](https://docs.composio.dev/toolkits/googlesuper) and [Github](https://docs.composio.dev/toolkits/github)

the final submission should be a visualized dependency graph where i can see connection (this is not super important just should exist for me to see if graph with edges and nodes)
